scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (G, B, D) plus safe extensions (A, E). Zero avoid notes. One of the most guitar-friendly pentatonic scales.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (G) and major 3rd (B) for resolution. Perfect for folk and rock guitar.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for G major in rock, country, blues, and folk. Extremely common. Ideal for memorable hooks.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental G major scale with one sharp (F#). Complete melodic freedom.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. C (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to B or D.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over G major. Essential for full diatonic expression.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises C to C# (#11) creating dreamy, ethereal quality.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (C#) for signature Lydian color.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Gmaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Perfect for film scores and jazz.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lowers F# to F (b7) creating dominant 7th character. Perfect for bluesy G major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The F (b7) creates bluesy edge. Great for rock and folk guitar.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for G7 chords and folk-rock vamps. Essential for jam bands.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. F# is a chord tone (maj7).
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (F#). All tones work beautifully.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Gmaj7. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (C#) avoids suspended sound. THE scale for Imaj7 in modern jazz.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (C#) is signature sound. Essential for Gmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz and fusion.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A Dorian is the natural ii mode in G major. Major 6th (F#) creates jazzier minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the F# (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Essential for jazz, pop, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. F# (13th) essential for ii-V-I.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: F# (13th) crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to D7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for Am7-D7-Gmaj7. The standard iim7 sound.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Phrygian is iii mode of G major. b2 (C) creates Spanish/exotic character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (C) for Phrygian color.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major is the natural scale for IV in G major. Creates subdominant function.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root (C) and major 3rd (E). F (11th) can sound suspended.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Perfect for folk and rock.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (F#) creates modern, floating IV sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (F#) creates bright, dreamy quality. Perfect for Cmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV sound. Perfect for jazz and fusion.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in G major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: D Mixolydian shares all notes with G major. b7 (C) creates dominant 7th character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The C (b7) creates dominant pull to G. Emphasize F# and C for strong V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in G major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. b7 (C) is a chord tone.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd (F#) and b7 (C) - the tritone that resolves to G.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (Am7-D7-Gmaj7).

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with G major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: E minor is vi of G major. Shares all seven notes. Most common minor chord in G major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All G major notes work perfectly. Natural minor sound in the key.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in G major. Perfect for G-D-Em-C progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Natural minor sound perfect for vi minor 7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. Standard vim7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in G major. Perfect for G-D-Em7-C progressions.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of Western music in G major. Perfect for guitar and folk music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, optimistic, and guitar-friendly. The most common folk progression.
famousSongs: Ring of Fire, Good Riddance (Time of Your Life), Brown Eyed Girl
commonProgressions.tips: G major is extremely guitar and folk-friendly. All three chords are open position.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop progression in G. The 'Axis of Awesome' progression.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile. Works for entire songs.
famousSongs: No Woman No Cry, With or Without You, Zombie
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely common in modern music. Can be played in any order.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic '50s progression in G. The doo-wop changes.
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro vibes. Can enhance with Gmaj7-Em7-Cmaj7-D7.

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: The fundamental jazz progression in G major.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated with strong resolution. The jazz standard.
commonProgressions.tips: Master this for jazz. Practice with extensions: Am9-D13-Gmaj9.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic folk/pop progression with smooth voice leading.
famousSongs: Common in folk and country music
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for acoustic guitar. Very singable.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop/rock starting on vi minor in G.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact.

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal rock progression in G borrowing bVII (F major).
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold, anthemic, powerful with modal flavor.
famousSongs: Sweet Child O' Mine, Hey Jude sections
commonProgressions.tips: The F major is borrowed from G Mixolydian. Creates powerful rock sound.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Double V creates urgency.
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. Repeated V emphasizes tension.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic folk and country progression in G.
commonProgressions.tips: Very stable - keeps returning to I. Perfect for acoustic styles.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression in G combining relative minor with ii-V-I.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Extends ii-V-I with vi start. Great for learning harmony.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Ascending progression in G through scale degrees.
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord (Bm) is underused but effective.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression in G - the rhythm changes.
commonProgressions.tips: Essential for jazz. Strong harmonic motion.
